cmseeddrill000000000000001.name: Straight cueing control
cmseeddrill000000000000001.description: A basic drill for a straight cue action, stable stance and center-ball contact without unwanted side spin.
cmseeddrill000000000000001.goal: Build a reliable straight delivery before potting and positional drills.
cmseeddrill000000000000001.rules: Place the cue ball on the brown line and a red in the center of the table. Play 20 shots at one tempo. After each shot, mark whether the cue ball left the line.
cmseeddrill000000000000001.successCriteria: At least 16 of 20 shots stay on line, with the cue ball remaining in the central corridor after contact.

cmseeddrill000000000000002.name: Long red to the corner
cmseeddrill000000000000002.description: Long potting practice with sighting control and a calm acceleration through the cue ball.
cmseeddrill000000000000002.goal: Improve the long-red percentage and remove forced cueing on distance shots.
cmseeddrill000000000000002.rules: Play the red to the far top corner from the diagram position. Complete 5 sets of 10 shots, fixing the contact point before each shot.
cmseeddrill000000000000002.successCriteria: At least 5 reds potted in every set, with stable stance and repeatable preparation time.

annotations.sight: Check the line from behind the cue ball

cmseeddrill000000000000003.name: Black with position on blue
cmseeddrill000000000000003.description: A positional drill: pot the black and bring the cue ball into a working zone for a continuation through blue.
cmseeddrill000000000000003.goal: Learn to control pace after the black and avoid leaving the cue ball tight to a cushion.
cmseeddrill000000000000003.rules: Play the black to the top right corner. After contact, the cue ball should return to the marked zone near the blue. Play 4 sets of 8 shots.
cmseeddrill000000000000003.successCriteria: At least 60% blacks potted and at least 50% cue-ball zone hits.

cmseeddrill000000000000004.name: Small break around the pink
cmseeddrill000000000000004.description: Break-building around pink and black to train next-ball choice, tempo and short cue-ball control.
cmseeddrill000000000000004.goal: Build a stable mini-break from reds around the pink while returning to pink or black.
cmseeddrill000000000000004.rules: Start from a chosen red near the pink. After each red, play a color and return to one of the marked zones. Work in runs of 6 reds.
cmseeddrill000000000000004.successCriteria: A 12+ point break in at least three of five attempts, without drifting the cue ball to a cushion after the color.

annotations.choice: Choose the next red before playing the color

cmseeddrill000000000000005.name: Safe baulk safety
cmseeddrill000000000000005.description: A classic safety: send the object ball up-table and bring the cue ball back into baulk behind the colors.
cmseeddrill000000000000005.goal: Build a reliable safety that leaves the opponent a long, difficult reply.
cmseeddrill000000000000005.rules: Play a thin contact on the red. The cue ball returns into the marked baulk zone via a cushion, and the red leaves an easy potting line.
cmseeddrill000000000000005.successCriteria: In 7 of 10 attempts the cue ball finishes in baulk and the opponent has no short direct pot.

annotations.thin: Thinner contact, less risk of leaving a pot

cmseeddrill000000000000006.name: One-cushion snooker escape
cmseeddrill000000000000006.description: Practice calculating a cushion angle and controlling speed when escaping from a snooker.
cmseeddrill000000000000006.goal: Hit the object ball reliably through one cushion and leave a safe reply.
cmseeddrill000000000000006.rules: The cue ball is blocked by colors. Calculate the angle from the bottom cushion and hit the red. Start without potting pressure, then add safety control.
cmseeddrill000000000000006.successCriteria: At least 8 red contacts from 10 attempts, with no more than two fouls from pace or a miss.

annotations.angle: Aim at the cushion point, not at the red

cmseeddrill000000000000007.name: Final black under pressure
cmseeddrill000000000000007.description: A repeatable deciding-black situation: routine, breathing and one quality shot under score pressure.
cmseeddrill000000000000007.goal: Make the pre-shot routine stable when the shot value is higher than in normal practice.
cmseeddrill000000000000007.rules: Call the score before each shot and complete the full routine. The run ends after a miss or after 10 consecutive pots.
cmseeddrill000000000000007.successCriteria: Best streak of 7+ final blacks with the same routine and no rushed preparation.

cmseeddrill000000000000008.name: 6 reds: break control
cmseeddrill000000000000008.description: A match-style mini-break with six reds, correct color choice and decision-error tracking.
cmseeddrill000000000000008.goal: Transfer technical skills into a game scenario: plan, execute and adjust after errors.
cmseeddrill000000000000008.rules: Set six reds as shown. Play by match rules: red, color, next red. After the run, record whether the stop came from a decision or execution error.
cmseeddrill000000000000008.successCriteria: Average 18+ points per attempt with a clear reason why each break ended.

annotations.review: After the break, record: decision or execution

cmseeddrill000000000000009.name: Long red with black-side recovery
cmseeddrill000000000000009.description: A professional long-red drill where the pot must immediately create a workable angle on black or pink.
cmseeddrill000000000000009.goal: Connect long potting with precise first-position control in a break-building situation.
cmseeddrill000000000000009.rules: Play the long red to the top corner. The cue ball must finish in the marked zone for black. Play 6 sets of 8 shots with a full pre-shot routine on every shot.
cmseeddrill000000000000009.successCriteria: At least 55% long reds potted and at least 45% cue-ball hits in the position zone.

annotations.routine: Pause on the line, then one tempo

cmseeddrill000000000000010.name: Three-ball black clearance
cmseeddrill000000000000010.description: A compact clearance pattern around the black where one wrong next-red choice immediately breaks the run.
cmseeddrill000000000000010.goal: Strengthen micro-position reading and short cue-ball control around the black.
cmseeddrill000000000000010.rules: Set three reds around the black. Play red, black, red, black, red, black. Repeat 10 runs, changing the first red.
cmseeddrill000000000000010.successCriteria: At least 18 points in 6 of 10 runs and no more than two positional errors in the drill.

annotations.choice: Next red chosen before playing the black

cmseeddrill000000000000011.name: Two-visit containment safety
cmseeddrill000000000000011.description: A tactical two-visit drill: the first shot limits options, the second locks table control.
cmseeddrill000000000000011.goal: Build safety sequences, not only single safety shots, with a predictable opponent reply.
cmseeddrill000000000000011.rules: Play a thin contact on the red and return the cue ball to baulk. On the second repeat, make the object ball finish near the long cushion while the cue ball is behind the colors.
cmseeddrill000000000000011.successCriteria: In 7 of 10 attempts the cue ball is hidden behind the colors, the object ball leaves no direct pot, and the tactical error is recorded immediately.

annotations.plan: Plan the opponent's reply before the first shot

cmseeddrill000000000000012.name: Two-cushion snooker escape
cmseeddrill000000000000012.description: A demanding escape: two-cushion contact must avoid the foul and leave a safe position.
cmseeddrill000000000000012.goal: Bring angle calculation and pace control to match level under penalty pressure.
cmseeddrill000000000000012.rules: The cue ball is blocked by colors. Calculate the two-cushion route to the red. After contact, the cue ball or red must finish in a safe sector.
cmseeddrill000000000000012.successCriteria: At least 7 contacts from 10, no more than two fouls, and at least 5 safe leaves.

annotations.speed: Speed matters more than contact thickness

cmseeddrill000000000000013.name: First chance: 8 reds
cmseeddrill000000000000013.description: A match simulation for the first open chance: eight reds, color selection and a logged reason for the break ending.
cmseeddrill000000000000013.goal: Improve conversion of the first open chance into a decisive scoring advantage.
cmseeddrill000000000000013.rules: Set eight reds as shown. Play as in a match until a miss or positional loss. After each attempt, record whether the break stopped because of decision or execution.
cmseeddrill000000000000013.successCriteria: Average 32+ points, at least 3 breaks of 40+ from 8 attempts, and a clear stop reason for each break.

annotations.review: After the break: decision or execution

cmseeddrill000000000000014.name: Shot-clock color clearance
cmseeddrill000000000000014.description: A final color clearance under a preparation time limit, without losing routine quality.
cmseeddrill000000000000014.goal: Keep a clear pre-shot routine when match tempo requires faster decisions.
cmseeddrill000000000000014.rules: Play yellow, green, brown, blue, pink and black. Each shot has 20 seconds of preparation. Repeat the clearance 8 times.
cmseeddrill000000000000014.successCriteria: At least 5 complete clearances from 8 and average preparation time in the target window without rushing the final shot.

supplements.title: Supplement periods

forms.quickSubtitle: Add data without hunting for the right form.

placeholders.title: E.g. coaching session
placeholders.name: Supplement or period name
placeholders.dosageNote: No advice, only the recorded fact
placeholders.reason: Why this period matters in the history

hints.type: The type keeps calendar filtering and reports fast.
hints.title: Short names are easier to scan in the month grid.
hints.startAt: Use the real start time to make daily load visible.
hints.endAt: Leave empty when the event has no exact duration.
hints.description: Add context: place, goal, opponent or important condition.
hints.date: This record belongs to one calendar day.
hints.sleepHours: Decimals are fine, for example 7.5 hours.
hints.mood: One phrase is enough to reveal patterns later.
hints.notes: Record observations that help a coach understand the day.
hints.name: Record the period name without medical conclusions.
hints.startDate: Date from which the period appears in the calendar.
hints.endDate: Leave empty while the period is still active.
hints.dosageNote: Record the fact, not advice or a prescription.
hints.reason: Neutrally describe why this period appears in the history.

ai.title: AI Coach
ai.subtitle: Weekly reports from training, matches and calendar factors.
ai.authRequired: Sign in to create and read AI reports.
ai.empty: No reports yet. Generate the first weekly summary.

profileRequired.title: Player profile required
profileRequired.description: AI reports are built from the player profile and linked data.

statusText.queued: The report is queued. This page will refresh the status automatically.
statusText.running: The worker is generating the report now.

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Sign in to your player or coach account.

register.title: Create account
register.subtitle: Create a player account. All data belongs to the player.

profile.title: Player profile
profile.subtitle: Core player details and current equipment.
profile.authRequired: Sign in to fill in the player profile.

placeholders.seasonGoal: Main season goal and success measure

hints.firstName: Used in reports, profile screens and address copy.
hints.lastName: Helps distinguish the player in long-term history.
hints.dateOfBirth: Adds age context for training and competition history.
hints.country: Two-letter country code, for example GB or UA.
hints.dominantHand: Adds technical context to drills and coach notes.
hints.level: Briefly describe the player's current level.
hints.seasonGoal: Make it measurable: event, break, ranking or skill.

equipment.title: Equipment
equipment.subtitle: Cue, tip, chalk and active periods.
equipment.empty: No equipment has been added yet.

placeholders.notes: What changed in feel or performance

hints.cueName: Use a clear name so the period is easy to find later.
hints.cueWeight: Enter a number when known, or leave empty.
hints.tipSize: Tip size in mm, for example 9.5.
hints.tipBrand: Brand/model helps connect equipment to cue action changes.
hints.tipChangeDate: Useful when checking dips after a tip change.
hints.extension: Only fill this when a different extension was really used.
hints.chalk: Chalk can also explain feel and error patterns.
hints.activeFrom: Equipment is treated as active from this date.
hints.notes: Record facts briefly without long analysis.

drills.title: Drill library
drills.subtitle: Training templates, metrics and starting table layouts.
drills.authRequired: Sign in to work with the drill library.
drills.empty: No drill templates have been added yet.

placeholders.name: Long red with position
placeholders.description: Briefly describe what the drill trains
placeholders.rules: Sets, positions and what to record
placeholders.successCriteria: For example, 6 of 10 with zone control

hints.name: Short names are easier to pick during practice.
hints.category: Category drives library grouping and reports.
hints.difficulty: Choose the level where this drill should become normal.
hints.visibility: Private is only yours; shared can be reused later.
hints.description: One or two sentences to remember the drill quickly.
hints.goal: Describe the training effect, not the execution steps.
hints.rules: Write it so a coach can run the drill without extra explanation.
hints.successCriteria: Make it measurable by attempts, zone control or break size.
hints.tags: Separate tags with commas, up to 12.

training.title: Training
training.subtitle: Sessions, drills and attempts for the training day.
training.authRequired: Sign in to log training sessions.

placeholders.title: Cue action and long reds
placeholders.goal: What should improve by the end

hints.title: The name should make the session easy to find later.
hints.sessionType: Type separates solo, match-prep and coached days.
hints.goal: One concrete goal works better than a long plan.
hints.mood: A short phrase helps compare attempt quality later.

execution.empty: No drills have been added yet.

matches.title: Matches
matches.subtitle: Manual match, frame and key-stat history.
matches.authRequired: Sign in to keep the match log.

profileRequired.title: Player profile required
profileRequired.description: Create a profile first so matches can be saved to the player history.

placeholders.sourceUrl: Match sheet or event page link
placeholders.frameNotes: Error, strong safety or deciding moment

hints.opponentName: Opponent history is grouped by this name.
hints.matchDate: Leave empty if the exact time is unknown.
hints.frameScore: Enter the final match score in frames.
hints.tournament: Context separates practice from competition matches.
hints.format: For example Best of 5 or race to 3.
hints.count: Count for the whole match or frame.
hints.percent: Percentage from 0 to 100 without the % sign.
hints.country: Country code or name, whichever is clearer in history.
hints.city: City helps connect matches with travel and fatigue.
hints.club: Venue or club when table conditions matter.
hints.videoUrl: A link makes later review faster.
hints.notes: Capture 1-3 observations for the next review.
hints.frameNumber: Leave empty if the API should assign the next number.
hints.winner: Keep not set when the frame is recorded without a result.
